advisor_reason: SpikePoint is a direct Event Cloud plus SNN predecessor in the advisor group's architectural chain leading toward SECNet.

title: TAB: Temporal Accumulated Batch Normalization in Spiking Neural Networks
survey_reason: TAB addresses temporal covariate shift in SNN training and includes DVS-CIFAR10 only as a benchmark, so it is SNN-side background rather than an intersection paper.
advisor_reason: Temporal normalization may inform an SNN branch, but it is not directly tied to Event Cloud processing.

title: Threaten Spiking Neural Networks through Combining Rate and Temporal Information
survey_reason: HART reveals rate-versus-timing vulnerabilities across SNN data types and supports a focused robustness discussion, but it does not develop event-camera processing.
advisor_reason: Adversarial attack design is outside the current SECNet extension path.

title: Towards Energy Efficient Spiking Neural Networks: An Unstructured Pruning Framework
survey_reason: SNN neuron and weight pruning provides useful efficiency evidence and cautions about dense-model redundancy, without event-camera integration.
advisor_reason: Its sparsification evidence is adjacent to lightweight deployment but not specific to SECNet.

title: Autaptic Synaptic Circuit Enhances Spatio-temporal Predictive Learning of Spiking Neural Networks
survey_reason: The autaptic STC neuron targets long-term spatiotemporal prediction and gradient flow in SNNs, serving as temporal-dynamics background only.
advisor_reason: Learnable long-memory neuron pathways offer a concrete alternative to SSM-style temporal modeling for discussion.

title: CLIF: Complementary Leaky Integrate-and-Fire Neuron for Spiking Neural Networks
survey_reason: CLIF directly addresses temporal-gradient vanishing in SNN neurons and is important training/dynamics background, but the abstract does not establish event-camera integration.
advisor_reason: CLIF is part of the advisor method chain and supplies a neuron-level mechanism relevant to possible SNN extensions of SECNet.

title: Enhancing Adversarial Robustness in SNNs with Sparse Gradients
survey_reason: Gradient-sparsity regularization supplies SNN robustness evidence and includes event-based datasets, but the method is generic rather than designed for event-camera processing.
advisor_reason: The adversarial-robustness objective is outside the present SECNet method direction.

title: EvGGS: A Collaborative Learning Framework for Event-based Generalizable Gaussian Splatting
survey_reason: EvGGS is an event-only 3D reconstruction and dataset paper; it can illustrate downstream event-camera capability but does not use SNNs.
advisor_reason: Its raw-event feedforward pipeline and dataset may support broader task evaluation, but the Gaussian-splatting method is distant from SECNet.

title: EvTexture: Event-driven Texture Enhancement for Video Super-Resolution
survey_reason: EvTexture demonstrates event-guided texture restoration as event-camera-side task background, without SNN or direct survey-intersection contribution.
advisor_reason: Video super-resolution texture restoration is not a plausible SECNet extension target from the current evidence.

title: High-Performance Temporal Reversible Spiking Neural Networks with $\mathcalO(L)$ Training Memory and $\mathcalO(1)$ Inference Cost
survey_reason: T-RevSNN addresses SNN training memory and inference cost through temporal reversibility, providing efficiency background without event streams.
advisor_reason: Its explicit training-memory and inference-cost design gives a useful scalable-SNN comparator for future hybrid exploration.

title: Learning Scale-Aware Spatio-temporal Implicit Representation for Event-based Motion Deblurring
survey_reason: SASNet is event-camera-side deblurring with continuous spatial-temporal scale modeling, useful as a task example but not an SNN contribution.
advisor_reason: Scale-aware image deblurring is too specialized and architecturally distant from SECNet.

title: NDOT: Neuronal Dynamics-based Online Training for Spiking Neural Networks
survey_reason: NDOT supplies an online alternative to BPTT for SNN temporal credit assignment; CIFAR10-DVS is benchmark evidence rather than event-specific method design.
advisor_reason: Memory-efficient online training is adjacent to a future SNN branch but not in the direct Event Cloud chain.

title: Robust Stable Spiking Neural Networks
survey_reason: The stability analysis of LIF perturbation dynamics supports SNN robustness foundations, but only image classification is evaluated.
advisor_reason: Its robustness framework does not connect to SECNet's representation or scalability goals.

title: Sign Gradient Descent-based Neuronal Dynamics: ANN-to-SNN Conversion Beyond ReLU Network
survey_reason: SignGD neuronal dynamics advances low-timestep ANN-to-SNN conversion beyond ReLU and belongs in conversion background, not event-camera integration.
advisor_reason: The conversion mechanism may define an SNN deployment alternative, while remaining outside the Event Cloud method chain.

title: SpikeZIP-TF: Conversion is All You Need for Transformer-based SNN
survey_reason: Exact Transformer-to-SNN conversion supports the survey's conversion taxonomy but has no event-camera component.
advisor_reason: It is a possible deployment comparator rather than a direct SECNet architectural relation.

title: Towards efficient deep spiking neural networks construction with spiking activity based pruning
survey_reason: Activity-based structured SNN pruning contributes general efficiency background, without event-camera processing or distinctive temporal-stream evidence.
advisor_reason: Structured sparsification may inform lightweight SNN variants but is not a direct method-chain paper.

title: Towards Efficient Spiking Transformer: a Token Sparsification Framework for Training and Inference Acceleration
survey_reason: STATA addresses timestep-aware token sparsification in Spiking Transformers and supplies efficiency evidence, but not event-camera integration.
advisor_reason: Token sparsification and temporal alignment are concrete scaling mechanisms worth comparing with SECNet's Event Cloud sampling.

title: A Benchmark Dataset for Event-Guided Human Pose Estimation and Tracking in Extreme Conditions
survey_reason: EHPT-XC is a formal event-camera benchmark for pose and tracking in extreme conditions, useful for task and dataset coverage but non-spiking.
advisor_reason: The hybrid extreme-condition benchmark offers a credible extension/evaluation setting for future SECNet work.

title: A generalized neural tangent kernel for surrogate gradient learning
survey_reason: The surrogate-gradient NTK gives rigorous theoretical background for SNN training, while being unrelated to event-camera data.
advisor_reason: Kernel theory for surrogate gradients does not materially support the current SECNet TPAMI direction.

title: Advancing Spiking Neural Networks for Sequential Modeling with Central Pattern Generators
survey_reason: CPG-based spike positional encoding supports general SNN sequential modeling but is not evaluated on event-camera streams.
advisor_reason: Hardware-friendly positional encoding is adjacent long-sequence inspiration rather than a direct SECNet relation.

title: Advancing Training Efficiency of Deep Spiking Neural Networks through Rate-based Backpropagation
survey_reason: Rate-based backpropagation is useful for scalable SNN training; CIFAR10-DVS is only one benchmark and the method is not event-specific.
advisor_reason: Its training-complexity reduction is a concrete scalable-SNN comparator for future hybrid work.

title: Continuous Spatiotemporal Events Decoupling through Spike-based Bayesian Computation
survey_reason: This work directly implements event-stream motion segmentation through SNN Bayesian computation and STDP, providing a distinctive fully spiking intersection mechanism.
advisor_reason: Its continuous event-stream inference offers a mechanistically different SNN comparator for extending Event Cloud tasks.

title: E-Motion: Future Motion Simulation via Event Sequence Diffusion
survey_reason: E-Motion is a non-spiking event-sequence forecasting task that broadens event-side temporal modeling but is peripheral to the strict intersection.
advisor_reason: Future-motion prediction is possible task inspiration but architecturally distant from SECNet.

title: EGSST: Event-based Graph Spatiotemporal Sensitive Transformer for Object Detection
survey_reason: EGSST preserves asynchronous events as graphs for efficient detection and is useful event-side representation background, but it is non-spiking.
advisor_reason: Graph construction and adaptive temporal activation are concrete comparators for Event Cloud processing.

title: EnOF-SNN: Training Accurate Spiking Neural Networks via Enhancing the Output Feature
survey_reason: ANN-guided output-feature learning is generic SNN training and uses neuromorphic datasets only for evaluation.
advisor_reason: Its hybrid representation offers limited inspiration for an SNN branch.

title: Event-3DGS: Event-based 3D Reconstruction Using 3D Gaussian Splatting
survey_reason: Event-3DGS supplies event-only 3D reconstruction and noise-handling evidence but does not involve SNNs.
advisor_reason: Gaussian-splatting reconstruction is outside the current SECNet method path.

title: EyeGraph: Modularity-aware Spatio Temporal Graph Clustering for Continuous Event-based Eye Tracking
survey_reason: EyeGraph provides event-camera tracking, dataset, and dynamic-graph representation evidence on the event side, without SNNs.
advisor_reason: Dynamic event graphs and an in-the-wild dataset are adjacent representation/evaluation ideas.

title: FEEL-SNN: Robust Spiking Neural Networks with Frequency Encoding and Evolutionary Leak Factor
survey_reason: FEEL-SNN develops frequency encoding and adaptive leak for generic SNN robustness, not event-camera processing.
advisor_reason: Frequency encoding directly overlaps a mechanism axis relevant to SECNet's frequency-aware modules.

title: LaSe-E2V: Towards Language-guided Semantic-aware Event-to-Video Reconstruction
survey_reason: LaSe-E2V is event-camera reconstruction background showing semantic priors, with no SNN contribution.
advisor_reason: Language-guided video reconstruction is outside SECNet's likely extension scope.

title: LM-HT SNN: Enhancing the Performance of SNN to ANN Counterpart through Learnable Multi-hierarchical Threshold Model
survey_reason: LM-HT contributes multi-threshold SNN and conversion theory but no event-stream-specific design.
advisor_reason: Reparameterizable threshold models may inform deployment but not the Event Cloud architecture.

title: Neuronal Competition Groups with Supervised STDP for Spike-Based Classification
survey_reason: Supervised STDP competition provides local-learning background for SNN classification, without event-camera evidence.
advisor_reason: The shallow classification mechanism is not materially related to SECNet.

title: PPLNs: Parametric Piecewise Linear Networks for Event-Based Temporal Modeling and Beyond
survey_reason: PPLNs are explicitly designed for event-camera temporal inference using membrane-potential-inspired functions, but the abstract does not establish that the network is an SNN.
advisor_reason: Its continuous parametric temporal representation is a useful non-SNN comparator across several event tasks.

title: QKFormer: Hierarchical Spiking Transformer using Q-K Attention
survey_reason: QKFormer is a major directly trained Spiking Transformer backbone but has no event-camera integration in the abstract.
advisor_reason: Its scalable binary attention is a strong SNN architecture comparator.

title: Rethinking the Membrane Dynamics and Optimization Objectives of Spiking Neural Networks
survey_reason: Learnable initial membrane potential and temporal objectives are useful SNN dynamics background; DVS datasets are benchmark-only.
advisor_reason: Neuron dynamics may support an SNN extension but have no direct Event Cloud connection.

title: Revisiting motion information for RGB-Event tracking with MOT philosophy
survey_reason: This RGB-event tracker shows how event motion cues solve temporal association, serving as non-spiking task background.
advisor_reason: Motion-aware tracking is a possible downstream extension, not a core method relation.

title: SpikedAttention: Training-Free and Fully Spike-Driven Transformer-to-SNN Conversion with Winner-Oriented Spike Shift for Softmax Operation
survey_reason: SpikedAttention is important Transformer-to-SNN conversion and energy background but does not use event cameras.
advisor_reason: It can serve as an SNN conversion/deployment comparator.

title: Spiking Neural Network as Adaptive Event Stream Slicer
survey_reason: SpikeSlicer directly uses an SNN to adaptively slice event streams with downstream ANN feedback, making the SNN-event interface itself the contribution.
advisor_reason: Adaptive event grouping and SNN-ANN cooperation directly align with SECNet's scalable Event Cloud processing questions.

title: Spiking Token Mixer: An event-driven friendly Former structure for spiking neural networks
survey_reason: STMixer is a hardware-compatible generic SNN architecture, not an event-camera method despite its event-driven terminology.
advisor_reason: Asynchronous-chip-compatible operators offer a concrete deployment comparator.

title: Spiking Transformer with Experts Mixture
survey_reason: Spiking MoE supplies sparse conditional-computation background but only generic neuromorphic/static benchmarks.
advisor_reason: Conditional computation is adjacent scaling inspiration.

title: Take A Shortcut Back: Mitigating the Gradient Vanishing for Training Spiking Neural Networks
survey_reason: Shortcut backpropagation addresses generic SNN gradient vanishing; dynamic datasets do not make it event-specific.
advisor_reason: Training stabilization may matter to an SNN branch but is not method-chain work.

title: Zero-Shot Event-Intensity Asymmetric Stereo via Visual Prompting from Image Domain
survey_reason: Zero-shot event-image stereo is non-spiking event-camera depth background with useful cross-modal transfer evidence.
advisor_reason: Cross-domain prompting may inspire transfer evaluation but is not central to SECNet.
